mission.title: crossplane: Allow deterministic Composite Resource Names
mission.description: Allow deterministic Composite Resource Names. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current crossplane deployment
steps.description:
Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What problem are you facing?

Crossplane composite resource names are generated by the kube API server through setting `generatedName` to `<claim-name>-`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow deterministic Composite Resource Names
steps.description:
Can this issue be addressed by remembering to set `crossplane.io/external-name` annotations on the managed resources that are derived from something deterministic (e.g. the claim name, or some formatting thereof)?

I think I'm almost following you here, but an example would help. It sounds like
```yaml
If the name is too long, it is trimmed:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow deterministic Composite Resource Names" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Can this issue be addressed by remembering to set `crossplane.io/external-name` annotations on the managed resources that are derived from something deterministic (e.g. the claim name, or some formatting thereof)?

I think I'm almost following you here, but an example would help.
codeSnippets: If the name is too long, it is trimmed:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
